headline: My life as a Russian exile in Paris: 'You can't fight French bureaucracy'
subtitle: 'A Russian in Paris' (1/6). Journalist Elizaveta Osetinskaya, who came to France in 2022 because of her opposition to the war in Ukraine, describes the difficulties she has faced with the prefecture while barely speaking the language.

Widespread drying of soil and rivers in France raises alarm among hydrologists
subtitle: Surface water and soil have been gripped by severe drought. While the situation for groundwater might be different, tensions are simmering over how water is being used.

headline: After exoplanets, the hunt for 'exomoons' begins
subtitle: An international team has identified an object orbiting a brown dwarf, which itself revolves around a star twice as small as our Sun. The findings were published Wednesday in the journal 'Nature.'

headline: ''No kids' policies promote a polarized and reductive vision of society'
subtitle: As child-free spaces continue to grow rapidly, a group of non-profit leaders, researchers and artists argue for Le Monde that the youngest members of society should not be seen as a nuisance to be tolerated, but as a valuable presence to be welcomed.

headline: How Bernard Arnault works to keep his media outlets in line
subtitle: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (4/6). Since the 1990s, the CEO of LVMH has built a small media galaxy in print and radio, which he uses to convey his ideas and tastes, but also to promote his brands.

How social media bans for young users, now in effect in France, have spread worldwide
subtitle: France has decided to ban those under 15 from major platforms such as Instagram and TikTok. The country is following a global trend that has gained momentum in recent years.

headline: Inside the court of France's richest man, Bernard Arnault
subtitle: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (1/6). The billionaire at the head of LVMH is as secretive as he is powerful. He relies on a devoted entourage in which a mix of fear and admiration prevails.

headline: Bernard Arnault's business-first strategy with foreign heads of state, from Trump to Putin
subtitle: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (3/6). The head of LVMH, which generates 70% of its revenue outside Europe, has fostered relationships with the leaders of the markets he targets. Foremost among them is the president of the United States, whom he has courted since the 1980s.
